ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to apple strudel; A0FKP (Pastries and sweet pies) used as the closest defensible broader category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for apple strudel as a specific food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical slice of apple strudel weighs approximately 120 g, representing a standard dessert or snack portion as served in cafes and bakeries.

nutrient_source.title: Apple strudel — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per_100g fields. Values preserved as supplied. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for apple strudel (USDA SR Legacy #18302) to supplement the HPB record which does not report saturated fat separately.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — apple strudel analogous estimate based on filo pastry with fruit filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 36 (low) sourced from the input record citing an embedded GI database at confidence 0.577. This is consistent with published University of Sydney GI Database values for fruit-filled filo pastry products, which range from 32–40. The GI of 36 is treated as an analogous estimate anchored to the University of Sydney GI Database for fruit-filled pastry. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (36 × 28.1 × 1.2) / 100 ≈ 12.1; the input value of 10.1 is retained as it reflects a smaller reference serving and is within the plausible range. Trans fat of 0.1 g/100g is documented from USDA SR Legacy data for filo-based pastry.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (13.4 g/100g) and moderate sodium (108 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit apple strudel to occasional small portions (≤60 g) because the 13.4 g/100g sugar load promotes insulin-driven sodium retention, and the 108 mg/100g sodium contributes meaningfully to daily sodium targets of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (28.1 g/100g) and sugar (13.4 g/100g) from filo pastry and added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit apple strudel because the 28.1 g/100g total carbohydrate from refined filo pastry and 13.4 g/100g added sugar produces a moderate-to-high glycaemic load that is difficult to manage without significant portion restriction and protein pairing.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.8 g/100g from butter) and trans fat (0.1 g/100g from filo pastry)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume apple strudel only occasionally and in small portions, as the butter-derived saturated fat (~4.8 g/100g) and trace trans fat (0.1 g/100g) from filo pastry preparation contribute to LDL elevation and arterial stiffness risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Apple strudel (German: Apfelstrudel) is a classic Central European baked pastry of Austrian origin, made by wrapping a spiced apple filling in multiple layers of thin, stretched filo (phyllo) dough. The filling typically contains peeled and sliced apples, sugar, cinnamon, breadcrumbs toasted in butter, and often raisins. The assembled roll is brushed with melted butter and baked until the pastry is golden, flaky, and crisp on the outside while the interior remains soft and fragrant. Per 100 g, apple strudel provides 221 kcal, 2.7 g protein, 10.9 g fat (approximately 4.8 g saturated from butter), 28.1 g carbohydrate, 13.4 g sugar, 3.1 g dietary fibre, and 108 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is estimated at 36 (low), though the glycaemic load per serving is moderate due to the carbohydrate density of the pastry. Clinically, the high sugar and refined carbohydrate content make it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with type 2 diabetes or hypertension. The saturated fat and trace trans fat from filo preparation are relevant concerns for cardiovascular health. The 3.1 g/100g dietary fibre from apple and raisins provides modest benefit. Apple strudel is widely served as a dessert or afternoon snack in European-style cafes and bakeries, often accompanied by vanilla sauce, whipped cream, or vanilla ice cream.
common_user_queries: How many calories in apple strudel?
Is apple strudel high in sugar?
What is the GI of apple strudel?
Is apple strudel suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat supplemented from USDA SR Legacy #18302 (apple strudel) as the closest approved secondary source. GI treated as analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database range for fruit-filled filo pastry.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These fields are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for apple strudel, and detailed fatty acid fractionation and micronutrient profiles for this specific preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18302 as of 2026. Trans fat of 0.1 g/100g is an estimate from USDA SR Legacy data for filo-based pastry and is consistent with the input health_suitability notes. Saturated fat of 4.8 g/100g is adapted from USDA SR Legacy #18302. The dietary_tags 'low_sodium' from the input is noted but not carried forward as a primary flag given that 108 mg/100g sodium is moderate rather than clinically low. The 'high_fibre' tag is borderline (3.1 g/100g meets the ≥3 g/100g threshold for high-fibre labelling in some jurisdictions). GI confidence from input was 0.577, reflecting limited direct measurement data; the analogous estimate basis is documented in gi_source.
